# Tomato Mien

> A desktop app for rule-based alarms and Pomodoro focus sessions.

Tomato Mien is a free, open-source, cross-platform desktop application that helps users manage flexible alarm rules and optional Pomodoro focus cycles. Built with Electron and React.

## Key Features

- Rule-based alarm scheduling with triggers and filters
- Interval alarms (e.g. every 15, 25, 30 minutes)
- Specific time alarms (e.g. 2:30 PM)
- Time range filters to restrict when alarms fire (e.g. 9 AM - 5 PM)
- Optional Pomodoro timer with 25-minute focus and 5-minute break cycles
- Pomodoro plugin toggle in Settings
- Background alarm evaluation via Web Worker
- Native desktop notifications with sound
- Rule management dashboard with search and sort
- Fully offline — no internet required
- Scheduled rule activation for future dates

## Tech Stack

- Electron (main process)
- React + TypeScript (renderer)
- Vite (build tool)
- Jotai (state management)
- Dexie / IndexedDB (local persistence)
- Tailwind CSS (styling)
- Web Worker (background alarm evaluation)

## Platforms

- macOS (.dmg)
- Windows (.exe)
- Linux (.AppImage)

## How to Use

1. Create a Rule — give your alarm rule a name
2. Add Triggers — choose interval or specific time
3. Set Filters (Optional) — restrict when alarms fire with time ranges
4. Enable & Go — toggle the rule on, alarms run in the background

## FAQ

- Is it free? Yes, MIT License, no ads or subscriptions.
- Offline? Fully offline, all data stored locally.
- Pomodoro? Enable it from Settings > Plugins, then restart the app.
- Background? Web Worker evaluates alarms even when minimized.
- Alarm types? Interval, specific time, multiple triggers, time range filters.
- Notifications? Native desktop notifications with sound, toggleable per rule.
